evals.prompt: I'm building a settings page for our SaaS app and need icons for: user profile, notifications, billing, security, and logout. We use React with fa-solid style throughout. Can you give me the complete icon plan with the JSX snippets?
evals.expected_output: A structured table mapping each settings item to a specific FA icon with full class names, JSX code snippets using @fortawesome/react-fontawesome, accessibility attributes, and consistency notes about using fa-solid throughout
expectations: Response includes icon recommendations for all 5 items: user profile, notifications, billing, security, and logout
Every recommended icon uses the fa-solid family prefix
JSX snippets use FontAwesomeIcon component from @fortawesome/react-fontawesome
Icons include accessibility attributes (aria-hidden, aria-label, or role)
Response includes a structured table or clear mapping format

evals.prompt: the loading spinner on my checkout button is jerky and too fast, its using fa-spinner fa-spin right now. how do i slow it down and make it smoother? also it needs to stop spinning when the payment goes through and show a checkmark instead
evals.expected_output: CSS custom property --fa-animation-duration to slow the spin, explanation of fa-spin vs fa-spin-pulse, and a code pattern for swapping fa-spinner to fa-check on success
expectations: Mentions --fa-animation-duration CSS custom property as the way to slow the spin
Provides code showing how to swap the spinner icon to a checkmark icon on payment success
Recommends fa-check or fa-circle-check as the success icon
Does not recommend writing custom @keyframes as the primary solution (prefers native FA utilities)

evals.prompt: We just migrated from Material Icons to Font Awesome 7 in our Vue 3 project but half the icons arent showing up. The console shows no errors. We installed @fortawesome/fontawesome-free via npm and imported 'all.css' in main.ts. Some icons work (like fa-solid fa-house) but others dont (fa-light fa-bell, fa-duotone fa-camera). What's going on?
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis that fa-light and fa-duotone require Font Awesome Pro, explanation of Free vs Pro families, and guidance on upgrading or using Free alternatives
expectations: Correctly identifies that fa-light and fa-duotone icon families require Font Awesome Pro
Explains that fontawesome-free only includes Solid, Regular, and Brands families
Suggests Free alternative icons (e.g., fa-regular fa-bell instead of fa-light fa-bell)
Mentions upgrading to @fortawesome/fontawesome-pro as an option
